retry_reason: Failure (returning false) indicates the bus is not registered at the moment of the call. The object may become registered later, but this function does not own the lifecycle; retry would be a caller-level policy decision.
contract_summary: Existence-check wrapper: temporarily acquires a reference on the bus's subsys_private via bus_to_subsys (a conditional get), records whether the pointer is non-NULL, then immediately releases the reference via subsys_put. The caller receives only a boolean indicating registration status and does not gain ownership of any object. The function is safe to call from any context and does not impose any locking or cleanup obligations on the caller. It is idempotent—calling it multiple times will consistently reflect the current state without side effects.
